is a [salsa](/wiki/Salsa_(dance) "Salsa (dance)") dance school academy in [Cali](/wiki/Cali "Cali"), [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), founded in the late 1990s by its current director, dancer and choreographer, Luis Eduardo Hernandez (also known as "El Mulato").[[1]](#cite_note-1)
["Q'Viva! With Jennifer Lopez, Marc Anthony & Jamie King"](http://qvivathechosen.com/). Qvivathechosen.com. Retrieved April 4, 2012.

["Grupa Swing Latino Tigo jest... – Video Dzień Dobry TVN"](http://dziendobry.tvn.pl/video/swing-latino-tigo,1,newest,4450.html). Dziendobry.tvn.pl. May 21, 2009. Retrieved April 4, 2012.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_deprecated_archival_service "Category:CS1 maint: deprecated archival service"))

["Un sueño llamado Salsa – Univision Novelas y Series"](https://web.archive.org/web/20120121230352/http://novelasyseries.univision.com/novelas-y-series-a-z/un-sueno-llamado-salsa). Novelasyseries.univision.com. September 14, 2010. Archived from [the original](http://novelasyseries.univision.com/novelas-y-series-a-z/un-sueno-llamado-salsa) on January 21, 2012. Retrieved April 4, 2012.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Salsa dance school academy in Cali, Colombia

|  |  |
| --- | --- |

Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Swing Latino"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Swing+Latino%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Swing+Latino%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Swing+Latino%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Swing+Latino%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Swing+Latino%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Swing+Latino%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(June 2017)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

**Swing Latino** is a [salsa](/wiki/Salsa_(dance) "Salsa (dance)") dance school academy in [Cali](/wiki/Cali "Cali"), [Colombia](/wiki/Colombia "Colombia"), founded in the late 1990s by its current director, dancer and choreographer, Luis Eduardo Hernandez (also known as "El Mulato").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

They are known for their appearance in the [Fox TV](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") show [¡Q'Viva! The Chosen](/wiki/Q%27Viva!_The_Chosen "Q'Viva! The Chosen").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Swing Latino has won multiple salsa dance contests in their home country Colombia and in the United States including the 2004 World Congress "Salsa Open" in [Philadelphia](/wiki/Philadelphia "Philadelphia"), the 2005 [World Salsa Dancing Federation's Championship](/wiki/World_Salsa_Championships#World_Salsa_Championships_/_World_Latin_Dance_Cup "World Salsa Championships"), in Miami and the 2006 and 2007 Las Vegas Salsa Championship, which aired in [ESPN](/wiki/ESPN "ESPN").

Swing Latino has also performed abroad in [Puerto Rico](/wiki/Puerto_Rico "Puerto Rico") and toured throughout the United Kingdom.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Swing_Latino&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Television")]

In 2009, Swing Latino was a guest performer in the Polish television show [You Can Dance: Po prostu tańcz!](/wiki/You_Can_Dance:_Po_prostu_ta%C5%84cz! "You Can Dance: Po prostu tańcz!") and the following day made another appearance on that same country's morning show [Dzień Dobry TVN](/wiki/Dzie%C5%84_Dobry_TVN "Dzień Dobry TVN").[[4]](#cite_note-4) Swing Latino also appeared on the Colombian [telenovela](/wiki/Telenovela "Telenovela") *Un Sueño Llamado Salsa* which aired in the United States on the Spanish language network [Telefutura](/wiki/Telefutura "Telefutura") in 2010.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
In 2012, after a visit from fellow salsa singer [Marc Anthony](/wiki/Marc_Anthony "Marc Anthony"), Swing Latino was featured as one of many Latin artists appearing in the television show *¡Q'Viva! The Chosen*, airing in both [Fox](/wiki/Fox_Broadcasting_Company "Fox Broadcasting Company") and [Univision](/wiki/Univision "Univision") networks.

Swing Latino appeared and competed on the first season of the 2017 reality program *[World of Dance](/wiki/World_of_Dance_(TV_series) "World of Dance (TV series)")*. They were the winners of the team division and competed in the finale against [Les Twins](/wiki/Les_Twins "Les Twins") and [Eva Igo](/wiki/Eva_Igo "Eva Igo").
